layers.title: Capture original task
layers.summary: This layer preserves the upstream request narrative that asked for client-side URMA data-plane failover. It is not runtime code, but it explains the intended feature scope that the rest of the PR implements.
layers.diff: +{"type": "user", "uuid": "u1", "parentUuid": null, "timestamp": "2026-06-11T09:00:37.000Z", "message": {"role": "user", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "We keep losing latency when a client's URMA (RDMA) data plane goes bad but the worker is still alive — the client stays pinned to the dead fast-path. Implement client-side failover: when the URMA data plane is persistently failing, switch the client to another worker. Add a UrmaSuccessRateTracker, two gflags (success-rate ratio + min sample count), wire it into the client worker API, and add a UT + ST."}]}}

layers.title: Document client knobs
layers.summary: Building on the captured feature intent, the docs add operator-facing descriptions for the two client runtime parameters. The documentation states hot-update behavior, valid ranges, and the disable semantics for a zero success-rate threshold.
layers.diff:
+## 客户端运行参数
+
+以下 gflag 参数可通过 `DATASYSTEM_CLIENT_CONFIG_PATH` 配置文件设置，支持热更新。
+
+| 序号 | 名称 | 默认值 | 取值范围 | 含义/用途 |
+|------|------|--------|----------|-----------|
+| 29 | `urma_failover_success_rate_ratio` | `0.5` | `[0.0, 1.0]` | Client 侧 URMA 数据面成功率阈值。一个完整统计窗口的成功率严格低于该 ratio 时，client 尝试切换 worker；设置为 `0.0` 表示关闭 URMA 故障切换功能，同时清空当前窗口状态。 |
+| 30 | `urma_failover_min_sample_count` | `5` | `> 0` | 每个 `client_dead_timeout_s` 统计窗口内进行故障切换判断前所需的最小 URMA 数据面样本数。样本不足时即使成功率很低也不会触发切换，避免少量请求误触发。 |

layers.title: Register build and flags
layers.summary: The first runtime layer makes the new tracker compile into both Bazel and CMake client builds, then exposes two dynamically monitored client flags. Validation rejects non-finite or out-of-range success ratios and a zero min-sample count, while `flags.h` allows both names through the client config monitor.
layers.diff:
+ds_cc_library(
+    name = "urma_success_rate_tracker",
+    srcs = [
+        "urma_success_rate_tracker.cpp",
+    ],
+    hdrs = [
+        "urma_success_rate_tracker.h",
+    ],
+    visibility = ["//visibility:public"],
+    deps = [
+        "//src/datasystem/common/flags:ds_flags",
+    ],
+    alwayslink = True,
+)
+        urma_success_rate_tracker.cpp
+DS_DEFINE_double(urma_failover_success_rate_ratio, 0.5,
+DS_DEFINE_uint32(urma_failover_min_sample_count, 5,
+DS_DEFINE_validator(urma_failover_success_rate_ratio, &ValidateUrmaFailoverSuccessRateRatio);
+DS_DEFINE_validator(urma_failover_min_sample_count, &ValidateUrmaFailoverMinSampleCount);

layers.title: Track unhealthy windows
layers.summary: On top of the registered knobs, the new tracker records per-window success and failure counts with atomics and settles a window only when a later request crosses the boundary. It gates failover on minimum samples, strict success-rate threshold, a switch-in-progress CAS, and capped failed-switch backoff that resets after a healthy full window.

layers.title: Expose internal callbacks
layers.summary: With the tracker in place, the common client-worker interface gains internal virtual hooks for recording URMA data-plane outcomes and finishing switch attempts. The remote implementation owns one tracker per worker API instance and invokes an installed callback when a settled window should switch workers.

layers.title: Record Set and Get outcomes
layers.summary: The next layer wires real request-path observations into the internal hooks. Set/Publish URMA writes record direct and pipelined UB write status, while Get records worker-to-client URMA error and TCP fallback payload cases so user-visible TCP fallback success can still count as a URMA data-plane failure.

layers.title: Switch workers asynchronously
layers.summary: Finally, ObjectClientImpl installs the callback on each worker API, submits the switch on the async worker pool, and verifies the reporting worker is still current before switching. The new `URMA_DATA_PLANE_FAILURE` reason keeps the current worker usable during failed switch attempts, preserving TCP fallback availability while another worker is sought.

layers.title: Add tracker unit tests
layers.summary: The unit-test layer first exercises the tracker as an isolated state machine. It covers full-window settlement, minimum sample gating, strict threshold comparisons, disable-and-clear behavior, duplicate-trigger suppression, failed-switch backoff, healthy-window reset, zero-window disablement, and concurrent boundary settlement.

layers.title: Add cluster failover ST
layers.summary: The system-test layer validates the tracker wiring against real KV traffic, service discovery, worker stop/start, client and worker inject points, and dynamic config updates. The scenarios distinguish local UDS paths from remote UB fallback, cover Set and Get failure directions, keep-current-worker behavior, backoff reset, hot flag updates, and local switch-back after recovery.
layers.diff:
+ds_cc_test(
+    name = "kv_client_urma_failover_test",
+    srcs = ["kv_client_urma_failover_test.cpp"],
+    tags = ["manual"],
+    deps = KV_COMMON_DEPS + [
+        "//src/datasystem/client:urma_success_rate_tracker",
+        "//src/datasystem/client:service_discovery",
+        "//src/datasystem/common/flags:ds_flags",
+    ],
+)
+
+TEST_F(KVClientUrmaFailoverTest, LocalDiscoveryUsesUdsAndDoesNotTriggerUrmaSwitch)
+TEST_F(KVClientUrmaFailoverTest, LEVEL1_RemoteUbFallbackSwitchByDiscovery)
+TEST_F(KVClientUrmaFailoverTest, LEVEL1_RemoteGetTcpFallbackSwitchByDiscovery)
+TEST_F(KVClientUrmaFailoverTest, LEVEL1_RemoteSwitchFailureKeepsCurrentWorkerAvailable)
+TEST_F(KVClientUrmaFailoverTest, LEVEL1_DynamicThresholdUpdateAppliesAtWindowSettlement)
+TEST_F(KVClientUrmaFailoverTest, LEVEL1_LocalFailRemoteFailThenSwitchBack)

summary: This PR adds client-side URMA data-plane health tracking that can asynchronously switch remote workers after persistently unhealthy URMA Set/Get windows while preserving TCP fallback availability and covering the behavior with docs, config validation, UTs, and STs.
